text.heading: Dangote, Otedola raise N155 million for mosque
text.p: Ilorin â€” The N1.5Billion fund raising ceremony of the Ilorin Central Jumaa't Mosque yesterday threw up the bickering between two business moguls, Otunba Femi Otedola of Zenon Oil and Alhaji Aliko Dangote of Dangote group.

Alhaji Dangote, the chief launcher at the ceremony had made a brief appearance with a whopping donation of 5% of the needed money ,N75M before he left the venue, but Femi Otedola a co-launcher shortly after, shocked the entire congregation with a donation of N80M, the highest, shooting Dangotes by N5M.
Over N600M was realized at the fund raising ceremony.

Meanwhile, President Umaru Musa Yar Adua at the ceremony charged religious faithful to use their religion to promote peace and development instead of acrimony and using religion to cause confusion among their subjects
According to him, " Islam is a religion of peace and should be strictly adhere with in order to move the nation forwardâ€?.

The president who was represented at the occasion by the Minister for Special Duties, Ibrahim Kazaure said that, Muslims should always use their religion to foster peace and harmony as this would assist the society to develop .

Yar Adua stressed that the present administration is committed to the sustenance of peace and development asking that all hands must be on deck towards maintaining the peace in order to accelerate the socio economic growth of the country..
He added that the efforts of the present government to develop agriculture is to boost food production to improve the social well being of the populace.

The president who noted that, an idle hand is a devils workshop emphasised that the current effort to develop the sector has assisted the government to reduce social vices among the teeming youths in the country.

Also, the chairman of the occasion, the Governor of Niger state, Dr. Babangida Aliyu said that, there was no doubt that the mosque deserve rehabilitation and called on the people invited for the launch to donate to the growth of the mosque.
Governor Bukola Saraki in his welcome address also believed that the mosque was long over due for rehabilitation and appealed to the people to donate to the mosque .
He however assured that, all the money realized at the launch would be judiciously utilized for the purpose it was meant for .
